# SDET Playwright Test Rules & Standards

> Extends [root AGENTS.md](../../AGENTS.md). Auto-loaded when editing Playwright E2E tests, shared test framework code, or test repo support files.
>
> **Framework**: `@gohighlevel/sdet-platform-core` (v3.0.68) — import as `@framework`.

---

## 1. File & Folder Structure

Every test module MUST follow this layout:

```
src/<module>/
├── objectRepository/    # Selectors only — CSS, XPath, IDs
├── pageRepository/      # Page objects — UI actions wrapping objectRepository
├── apiRepository/       # API client classes — setup, teardown, data fetch
│   └── apiConfig.ts     # Module-specific apiPaths object
├── helperRepository/    # Module-specific utilities
├── fixtures/            # Playwright fixtures composing the above
├── testData/            # Static test data and builders
└── tests/
    ├── api/             # API-only tests (sanity, regression, smoke)
    ├── ui/              # Browser-based UI tests
    └── publicApi/       # Public API endpoint tests
```

**Rules:**

- [MUST] Keep selectors in `objectRepository` — never hardcode locators in test specs.
- [MUST] Keep API setup/teardown in `apiRepository` — never in page objects.
- [MUST] Keep UI interactions in `pageRepository` wrapping `UIActions` or `FrameWebActions`.
- [SHOULD] Keep module-specific helpers in `helperRepository`.
- [SHOULD] Use `apiConfig.ts` per module for endpoint paths; import shared paths from `@common/apis/apiPaths`.
- See [playwright-pom](references/playwright-pom.md) for full POM reference.

---

## 2. Imports & Framework Usage

### Framework primitives (from `@framework`)

| Class | Purpose | Instantiation |
|-------|---------|---------------|
| `ApiClient` | HTTP calls (GET/POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE) with retry | `new ApiClient({ platform, prodEnv, customData })` |
| `UIActions` | UI element interactions (click, fill, hover, wait) | `new UIActions(page)` |
| `FrameWebActions` | iframe/nested frame interactions | `new FrameWebActions(page, frameLocator)` |
| `CommonUtils` | File, CSV, JSON, schema validation utilities | Static methods — no instantiation |
| `Fakers` | Test data generation (names, emails, phones, etc.) | `new Fakers()` |
| `DateHelper` | Date/time manipulation, timezone conversion | `new DateHelper()` |
| `SecretHandler` | Google Cloud Secret Manager access | `new SecretHandler()` |
| `ProjectHelper` | Login, navigation, network/console log capture | `new ProjectHelper(page)` |
| `AccessibilityHelper` | WCAG/axe-core scanning | Static methods |
| `UIPerformanceHelper` | FCP, LCP, performance metrics | Instance methods |
| `Retry` | Enum: `None`, `StatusCode`, `Body` | Used in ApiClient retry config |
| `Platform` | Enum: `AppEngine`, `MarketPlace`, `Public` | Used in ApiClient meta |

### Import conventions

```typescript
// Framework
import { test, expect, CommonUtils, Fakers, Retry, Platform, DateHelper, UIActions } from '@framework';

// Or from a fixture (preferred when fixture provides dependencies)
import { test, expect } from '@common/fixtures/common.fixture';

// Config
import testConfig from '@root/testConfig';

// API paths
import { apiPaths } from '@common/apis/apiPaths';

// Module APIs, pages, objects
import { CalendarApis } from '@root/src/scheduling/pageFactory/apiRepository/Calendar.apis';
import { Widget } from '@root/src/scheduling/pageFactory/pageRepository/Widget';
import { AppointmentWidgetObjects } from '@root/src/scheduling/pageFactory/objectRepository/AppointmentWidget.Objects';
```

- [MUST] Import `test` and `expect` from the same source (either `@framework` or your fixture).
- [SHOULD] Prefer fixture imports when your test uses fixture-provided dependencies.
- [MUST] Import `Retry` and `Platform` enums from `@framework`, not define your own.
- See [shared-test-core](references/shared-test-core.md) for full framework reference.

---

## 3. Fixtures

### Fixture hierarchy

```
@framework (base Playwright test + expect)
  └── common.fixture.ts → provides: contactApis, fakers, dateHelper, uiActions, commonUtils, fscHelper
        ├── workflow.fixture.ts → adds: workflowApis, commonSteps, ...
        ├── workflowUI.fixture.ts → adds: workflowUI (shared browser session)
        ├── rentals.fixture.ts → adds: rentalsApis, rentalTestHelper, freeSlotsHelper
        └── <your-module>.fixture.ts
  └── Direct @framework extend (for simple API-only fixtures)
```

### Writing a fixture

```typescript
import type { CommonFixtures } from '@common/fixtures/common.fixture';
import { test as base, expect } from '@common/fixtures/common.fixture';

export type MyModuleFixtures = CommonFixtures & {
  myModuleApis: MyModuleApis;
  myHelper: MyHelper;
};

export const test = base.extend<MyModuleFixtures>({
  myModuleApis: async ({}, use) => {
    const apis = new MyModuleApis();
    await use(apis);
  },
  myHelper: async ({ dateHelper }, use) => {
    // Fixtures can depend on other fixtures
    const helper = new MyHelper(dateHelper);
    await use(helper);
  },
});

export { expect };
```

**Rules:**

- [MUST] Extend from `common.fixture` (or `@framework` for API-only fixtures with no shared setup).
- [MUST] Preserve parent fixture types in your union type (`CommonFixtures & { ... }`).
- [MUST] Re-export `expect` from your fixture file.
- [SHOULD] Use `common.fixture` when tests need contactApis, fakers, dateHelper, or uiActions.
- [SHOULD] For UI fixtures with shared browser sessions, use worker-scope setup (see `workflowUI.fixture.ts` pattern).
- [MUST] Avoid creating parallel infrastructure for things `@framework` already provides.

---

## 4. Test Structure

### Describe block

```typescript
test.describe.configure({ mode: 'default' }); // or 'serial' if tests depend on each other

/**
 * @coverage
 * - POST /calendars/ - Creates calendar with valid payload - Returns 200
 * - POST /calendars/ - Missing required field - Returns 400
 * - DELETE /calendars/{id} - Existing calendar - Returns 200
 */
test.describe(
  'Calendar CRUD operations',
  {
    tag: ['@AUTOMATIONS_SCHEDULING-SANITY', '@CALENDARS'],
    annotation: [{ type: 'module', description: 'Calendar CRUD' }],
  },
  () => {
    // shared state, lifecycle hooks, tests
  }
);
```

### Shared state & lifecycle

```typescript
test.describe('Calendar CRUD operations', { tag: [...] }, () => {
  let calendarApis: CalendarApis;
  let calendarId: string | undefined;
  const scheduleIds: string[] = [];

  test.beforeAll(async () => {
    calendarApis = new CalendarApis();
  });

  test.afterEach(async () => {
    // Cleanup in reverse creation order
    for (const id of scheduleIds) {
      try {
        await schedulesApis.deleteSchedule(id);
      } catch (err) {
        console.error(`Cleanup failed for schedule ${id}:`, err);
      }
    }
    scheduleIds.length = 0;

    if (calendarId) {
      try {
        await calendarApis.deleteCalendar(calendarId);
      } catch (err) {
        console.error(`Cleanup failed for calendar ${calendarId}:`, err);
      }
      calendarId = undefined;
    }
  });

  test('User creates a calendar with valid payload', async () => {
    // ...
  });
});
```

### Test body with `test.step()`

```typescript
test('User creates calendar and verifies free slots', async ({ fakers, dateHelper }) => {
  let calendarId: string;

  await test.step('Create calendar with team availability', async () => {
    const payload = buildCalendarPayload(fakers);
    const response = await calendarApis.createCalendar(payload);
    expect(response.code).toBe(200);
    calendarId = response.body.calendar.id;
  });

  await test.step('Verify free slots are returned', async () => {
    test.info().annotations.push(
      { type: 'calendarId', description: calendarId },
      { type: 'timezone', description: timezone },
    );

    const response = await calendarApis.getFreeSlots(calendarId, timezone);
    expect(response.code).toBe(200);
    expect(Array.isArray(response.body.slots)).toBe(true);
    expect(response.body.slots.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
  });
});
```

**Rules:**

- [MUST] Use `test.step()` with verb-first names: "Create appointment", "Verify response structure", "Delete calendar".
- [MUST] Use `@coverage` JSDoc block at describe-level documenting what the suite covers.
- [MUST] Use `test.describe.configure({ mode: 'serial' })` only when tests genuinely depend on each other.
- [SHOULD] Use `test.use({ trace: 'off' })` for API-only test files to reduce CI trace storage.
- [MUST] Never commit `test.only()` — it blocks CI for other suites.

---

## 5. Tags & Traceability

### Test case tags

All tags — including `@C{number}` — go in the describe block's `tag` array, NOT in test titles:

```typescript
test.describe('SaaS sub-account creation', {
  tag: ['@C10620', '@SubAccount', '@Agency', '@SaaS', '@AUTOMATIONS_SCHEDULING-SMOKE'],
  annotation: [{ type: 'module', description: 'SaaS sub-account creation' }],
}, () => {
  test('User creates and verifies SaaS sub-account', async () => { ... });
});
```

Test titles stay clean and descriptive — no tags in titles.

### Runtime annotations

```typescript
test.info().annotations.push(
  { type: 'locationId', description: locationId },
  { type: 'contactId', description: contactId },
  { type: 'calendarId', description: calendarId },
);
```

**Rules:**

- [MUST] Include `@C{number}` tag mapping to hl-test-manager for every managed test.
- [MUST] Use stable module tags (e.g., `@AUTOMATIONS_SCHEDULING-SANITY`, `@CONTACTS_API`) for CI filtering.
- [SHOULD] Add `test.info().annotations` for runtime-generated IDs after data is created.
- [MUST] Use camelCase variable names as annotation `type` — use actual variable names, no prefixes.
- [MUST] Use a single `push()` call with multiple annotations, not multiple calls.
- See [test-case-traceability](references/test-case-traceability.md) for full reference.

---

## 6. API Testing Patterns

### ApiClient usage

```typescript
const api = new ApiClient({
  platform: Platform.MarketPlace,
  prodEnv: testConfig.isProd,
  customData: { locationId: testConfig.locationId },
});

// Simple GET
const response = await api.get(
  CommonUtils.constructUriPath(apiPaths.calendar.CalendarID, calendarId),
);

// GET with retry
const response = await api.get(
  CommonUtils.constructUriPath(apiPaths.calendar.CalendarID, calendarId),
  { timeout: 10000 },
  {
    retries: 3,
    waitFor: Retry.Body,
    pollTime: 1000,
    searchParams: { path: 'calendar.id', value: calendarId },
  },
);

// POST with payload
const response = await api.post(apiPaths.calendar.Calendars, payload);
```

### API wrapper class pattern

```typescript
export class CalendarApis {
  private api: ApiClient;
  fakers = new Fakers();

  constructor() {
    this.api = new ApiClient({
      platform: Platform.MarketPlace,
      prodEnv: testConfig.isProd,
      customData: { locationId: testConfig.locationId },
    });
  }

  async createCalendar(payload: object) {
    const response = await this.api.post(apiPaths.calendar.Calendars, payload);
    expect(response.code).toBe(200);
    return response;
  }

  async getCalendar(calendarId: string) {
    return await this.api.get(
      CommonUtils.constructUriPath(apiPaths.calendar.CalendarID, calendarId),
    );
  }

  async deleteCalendar(calendarId: string) {
    const response = await this.api.delete(
      CommonUtils.constructUriPath(apiPaths.calendar.CalendarID, calendarId),
    );
    expect(response.code).toBe(200);
    return response;
  }
}
```

### Path construction

All endpoints MUST be referenced from `apiConfig.ts` or `apiPaths.ts` — never hardcoded as inline strings.

```typescript
// ✅ Always reference from apiPaths
CommonUtils.constructUriPath(apiPaths.calendar.CalendarID, calendarId);

// ✅ Query params — append to apiPaths reference
CommonUtils.constructUriPath(apiPaths.calendar.freeSlots, calendarId)
  + `?timezone=${encodeURIComponent(timezone)}&startDate=${startDate}`;

// ❌ WRONG — hardcoded endpoint string
CommonUtils.constructUriPath('/calendars/{calendarId}', calendarId);
api.get(`/calendars/${calendarId}`);
```

---

## 7. Assertions

### Order: status code → structure → values

```typescript
// 1. Status code
expect(response.code).toBe(200);

// 2. Structure
expect(Array.isArray(response.body.slots)).toBe(true);
expect(response.body.slots.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);

// 3. Values — use exact matching against input
expect(response.body.calendar.name).toBe(inputName);
expect(response.body.calendar.locationId).toBe(testConfig.locationId);

// 4. Nested/array validation
for (const slot of response.body.slots) {
  expect(slot).toHaveProperty('startTime');
  expect(['AVAILABLE', 'UNAVAILABLE']).toContain(slot.status);
}
```

**Rules:**

- [MUST] Assert status code first, always with `toBe(expectedCode)`.
- [MUST] Use `toBe(inputValue)` for user-set fields — never `toBeDefined()`.
- [SHOULD] Use `toEqual()` for deep object comparison.
- [SHOULD] Use `toContain()` for enum/set membership checks.
- [MUST] Guard optional fields: `if (response.body.field) { expect(...) }`.
- [SHOULD] Validate schema with `CommonUtils.validateSchema(schema, data)` for complex responses.

---

## 8. Cleanup & Data Isolation

```typescript
test.afterEach(async () => {
  if (resourceId) {
    try {
      // Neutralize server-side guards before deletion
      await api.updateResource(resourceId, { isDefault: false, calendarIds: [] });
      await api.deleteResource(resourceId);
    } catch (err) {
      console.error(`Cleanup failed for resource ${resourceId}:`, err);
    }
    resourceId = undefined;
  }
});
```

**Rules:**

- [MUST] Wrap all cleanup in `try/catch` with `console.error` — never `.catch(() => {})`.
- [MUST] Use `for...of` for async cleanup loops — never `forEach` with async callbacks.
- [MUST] Reset state variables after cleanup (`= undefined`, `.length = 0`).
- [SHOULD] Neutralize server-side guards (e.g., `isDefault: false`) before attempting deletion.
- [SHOULD] Clean up in reverse creation order.
- [SHOULD] Suppress expected 404s in cleanup (resource already deleted).

---

## 9. UI Test Patterns

### Page object usage

```typescript
test('User books appointment on widget', async () => {
  await widget.navigateToUnassignedCalendar(calendarId);
  await widget.selectTimezone(testConfig.locationTimeZone);
  const startTime = await widget.selectFirstAppointmentSlot();
  await widget.clickOnSelectDateOrContBtn();
  await widget.fillDefaultForm();
  appointmentID = await widget.clickOnBookAppointmentBtn();

  // Verify via API (not UI)
  const response = await appointmentApis.getAppointment(appointmentID);
  expect(response.appointmentStatus).toBe('confirmed');
});
```

### Object repository pattern

```typescript
// objectRepository/Calendar.Objects.ts — selectors only
export class CalendarObjects {
  CALENDAR_NAME_INPUT = '#calendar-name';
  SAVE_BUTTON = 'button[data-testid="save-calendar"]';
  // Dynamic selectors use REPLACE placeholder
  CALENDAR_ROW = `//tr[contains(.,'REPLACE')]`;
}

// pageRepository/CalendarPage.ts — actions wrapping selectors
export class CalendarPage {
  private objects = new CalendarObjects();
  private uiActions: UIActions;

  constructor(page: Page) {
    this.uiActions = new UIActions(page);
  }

  async fillCalendarName(name: string) {
    await this.uiActions.fillTextBox(this.objects.CALENDAR_NAME_INPUT, name);
  }

  async clickSave() {
    await this.uiActions.click(this.objects.SAVE_BUTTON);
  }

  async selectCalendarRow(name: string) {
    const selector = CommonUtils.replaceSelector(this.objects.CALENDAR_ROW, name);
    await this.uiActions.click(selector);
  }
}
```

### iframe handling

```typescript
import { FrameWebActions, UiOperationsMicroFrontend } from '@framework';

// Single iframe
const frameActions = new FrameWebActions(page, 'iframe#calendar-app');
await frameActions.clickFrameElement('.submit-btn');

// Nested iframes
const nestedFrame = new FrameWebActions(page, ['iframe#outer', 'iframe#inner']);
await nestedFrame.fillTextFrameElement('#name', 'Test');
```

**Rules:**

- [MUST] Use `UIActions` or `FrameWebActions` wrappers — never raw `page.click()` / `page.fill()`.
- [MUST] Use `CommonUtils.replaceSelector()` for dynamic selectors — never string concatenation.
- [SHOULD] Verify API responses after UI actions (not just visual checks).
- [SHOULD] Use `test.use({ trace: 'retain-on-failure' })` for UI tests.

---

## 10. Naming Conventions

### Test titles

Verb-first, user-centric, descriptive:

```typescript
// ✅ Good
'User creates a class booking calendar with team availability'
'User checks if appointment per slot is 1 when there are 2 team members'
'User lists calendars for the location and top calendars show correct active status'

// ❌ Bad
'calendar creation test'
'test case 42'
'should work correctly'
```

### test.step() names

Verb-first, action-oriented:

```typescript
await test.step('Create recurring teams calendar and schedule', async () => {});
await test.step('GET recurring-slots and verify response structure', async () => {});
await test.step('Verify slots match expected recurrence', async () => {});
await test.step('Delete calendar and confirm 404', async () => {});
```

### File naming

```
<descriptiveName>.test.ts           # All test files
<ModuleName>.apis.ts                # API client classes
<ModuleName>.Objects.ts             # Object repositories
<ModuleName>.ts or <ModuleName>Page.ts  # Page repositories
<moduleName>.data.ts                # Test data
<moduleName>.fixture.ts             # Fixtures
```

---

## 11. Anti-Patterns (Never Do)

| Anti-Pattern | Correct Alternative |
|---|---|
| `.catch(() => {})` silent swallow | `try/catch` + `console.error` |
| `forEach` with async callbacks | `for...of` loop |
| `test.only()` in committed code | Remove before commit |
| `toBeDefined()` for user-set fields | `toBe(inputValue)` |
| Hardcoded dates/timestamps | `DateHelper` or `Fakers` |
| `as any` type casts | Proper TypeScript types |
| Selectors in test specs | `objectRepository` classes |
| API setup in page objects | `apiRepository` classes |
| `page.click()` / `page.fill()` directly | `UIActions` wrappers |
| Hardcoded endpoint strings (`'/calendars/'`) | Reference from `apiPaths` (`apiPaths.calendar.Calendars`) |
| Multiple `annotations.push()` calls | Single `push()` with array |
| `test.skip()` without reason | `test.skip(condition, 'REASON: ...')` |
| Parallel infrastructure for secrets/reporting | `@framework` SecretHandler/ReportGenerator |

---
